notes: Initial model: keep+retention+z_score+novelty+cognitive_load+net_novelty+vz_score
Replacing retention with retention_per_sec hurts — rps has negative weight alone
retention + retention_per_sec together: rps captures duration confound while retention keeps raw signal
Midpoint retention from retentionCurve. r=+0.29 vs log_views. Best individual curve signal
Retention curve variance. r=-0.32 vs log_views (lower variance = more views)
Average of last 5 curve points. r=+0.30 vs log_views
All slope signals collinear with retention already in model — negative delta
r=+0.84, r_partial=+0.82. STRONGEST new signal. Log10 of first-7-day view sum predicts total views even after controlling for keep. Early algorithmic push is the mechanism.
r=+0.41, r_partial=+0.36. Counter-intuitive: BIGGER max retention drops = MORE views. Dramatic narrative turns drive virality, not smooth retention.
r=-0.32, r_partial=-0.26. LOWER retention variation = more views. Smooth, consistent watch patterns get algorithmic reward.
r=+0.32, r_partial=+0.25. Retention at 75% mark predicts views beyond keep. Deep viewer engagement matters independently.
r=+0.38, r_partial=+0.24. 10-second retention mark predicts views. First 10s = algorithmic fate.
r=+0.29, r_partial=+0.24. Midpoint retention predicts views. Videos maintaining middle engagement outperform.
r=+0.29, r_partial=+0.21. (retention-70)*duration = total watch-minutes above baseline. Algorithm rewards watch time volume.
r=+0.22, r_partial=+0.21. Higher sub retention % predicts views. Even loyal audience engagement matters.
r=+0.28, r_partial=+0.20. Non-subscriber retention predicts views. Cold traffic retention = virality signal.
r=-0.30, r_partial=-0.19. Lower like/view ratio = MORE views. Viral videos reach beyond core audience.
r=+0.24, r_partial=+0.18. Later peak day in first 30 days = more views. Sustained/second-wave virality indicator.
r=+0.24, r_partial=+0.17. Bigger 0-3s drop = more views (counter-intuitive). High initial spike height matters more than drop.
r=+0.14, r_partial=+0.15. Flatter 3-10s slope = more views. Hook retention in seconds 3-10 predicts.
r=+0.01, r_partial=+0.14. Later max drop = more views. Early max drops = hook failure.
r=+0.03, r_partial=+0.10. Subs gained per 10k views weakly predicts. Quality signal to algorithm.
r=-0.38, r_partial=-0.37. Viral videos DON'T peak day 1. Lower day-1 share = more total views. Algorithmic sustained push signal.
r=+0.34, r_partial=+0.31. Day7/Day1 view ratio predicts total views. Sustained momentum is the key to virality.
r=-0.39, r_partial=-0.28. Counter-intuitive: lower hook hold ratio = more views. Big initial spike height (replays) matters more than keeping it.
r=+0.34, r_partial=+0.26. Retention at 75%/25% predicts views. The narrative middle separates viral from average.
r=+0.11, r_partial=+0.23. Rougher retention curves = more views. Dramatic pacing creates engagement through variation.
r=+0.35, r_partial=+0.23. Curve area * duration = total watch seconds per viewer. Algorithm rewards watch time volume.
r=+0.28, r_partial=+0.22. Second week vs first week view ratio. Second-wave videos outperform.
r=+0.32, r_partial=+0.19. Higher initial retention spike (>1.0 = replays) predicts views. Replay behavior = strong hook.
r=-0.30, r_partial=-0.19. Lower weighted engagement per view = more views. Virality dilutes per-view engagement as audience broadens.
r=+0.18, r_partial=+0.18. Slower exponential retention decay predicts views. Captures overall curve shape quality.
r=+0.34, r_partial=+0.32. body_hold*10 + d7d1 - retVar*5. Best multi-signal combo found, captures 3 independent dimensions.
46-60s = sweet spot (28/98 hit 10M+, 2.9M median). 15-30s = death zone (0/11, 513K median). 121s+ small sample but high avg.
Best content combo: vz_type=B + z_type=C. Median 8.2M (3x avg). Visual curiosity gap + challenge/outcome text framing.
Higher swipe rate = more views (26% high-view vs 12% low-view). But r_partial=0.001 — pure consequence of reaching broader audiences.
r_partial=-0.27. Lower RPM = more views after controlling keep. Viral videos get pushed to less-monetizable audiences.
medium_decay=2.8M median vs fast_decay=413K. 7x difference. Curve shape alone separates winners.
'Making' = $24M avg (23 videos). Build/creation content is strongest format. 'Shoes' $11.5M, 'testing' $14M.
LLM vision score 1-10. r=+0.21 vs views but hurts regression (noise). r=-0.02 vs keep
LLM vision score 1-10. r=+0.30 vs keep but too noisy for regression
LLM vision score 0/1. 80% have faces. r=+0.12 vs views but hurts model
Duration buckets don't help. Only 10 shorts in dataset. Long videos R²=0.50 in stratified model
Non-linear keep effect (keep²). Captures accelerating returns at high keep rates
keep×vz interaction. Individually kept but doesn't add on top of keep_sq (+0.001)
log10(duration). Below threshold (+0.009)
keep+retention-100. Perfectly collinear with existing features
r=+0.39, r_partial=+0.38. TOP SIGNAL. StdDev of retention curve derivative = 'dramatic pacing'. Varied rate-of-change beats monotonic decay.
r=-0.11, r_partial=-0.24. More negative mean derivative = more views. Viral videos decay faster because they start higher.
r=+0.02, r_partial=-0.18. Viral videos still losing viewers at end, not flattening. Tension maintained through finale.
r=+0.19, r_partial=+0.18. More transcript sentences = more views. Narrative density matters.
r=-0.23, r_partial=-0.15. No recovery needed after cliff. Committed audience stays regardless.
r=+0.19, r_partial=+0.12. More words = more views. Content density signal.
Biggest single-point retention drop in curve. r=+0.41 vs log_views. Signals initial virality spike
Mean abs 2nd derivative. r=+0.12. Collinear with max_cliff — doesn't add on top
75th percentile retention. Doesn't add >0.01 on top of max_cliff
Tail std dev. Doesn't add incrementally
Early/late retention ratio. Individually kept but collinear with max_cliff
Drop from peak to first minimum. Below threshold
r_partial=+0.42. BEST SINGLE RATIO. deriv_std/retentionVariation = 'dramatic pacing efficiency'. Sharp turns at specific moments + smooth overall arc.
r_partial=+0.40. body_hold × (1-d1_share). Need BOTH mid-video retention AND sustained trajectory.
r_partial=+0.38. keep × deriv_std. Hook + dramatic pacing interaction outperforms either alone.
r=0.61, r²=0.37. z(keep)+z(deriv_std)+z(d7d1) explains 37% of view variance. Best 3-signal model found.
Non-linear: <75% keep = 3-12% hit 10M+. 75-80% = 25%. 80-85% = 41%. Marginal keep above 75 doubles hit rate.
r_partial=+0.32. vz_score × deriv_std. Visual curiosity + pacing = pre-production predictable virality.
Non-linear midpoint retention effect. Retained incrementally on top of sub_ret_gap

MILESTONE: CV R²>0.50! 13 features, CV=0.506±0.017, holdout=0.702, multiplier=2.6x. From 0.147→0.506 (+244%)
r=+0.37, r_partial=+0.34. Day3-7 avg / Day1 views. Algorithmic acceleration ratio. OUTLIER: 30.4x top10 vs 0.7x bottom10.

r_partial=+0.48 (370), +0.27 (203). Excess kurtosis of retention curve. Top10 avg=5.17 vs bottom10=2.10 (2.47x ratio).
r=-0.37, r_partial=-0.34. Viral videos DON'T follow exponential decay. Lower R² of log-linear fit = more views.
r=+0.46, r_partial=+0.43. BEST NEW COMBO: derivStd × bodyHold × (1-decayR2). Three independent dimensions combined.
r=+0.46, r_partial=+0.40. prev_views × maxCliff interaction. Channel momentum amplifies dramatic content.
r=+0.42, r_partial=+0.38. lateEarlyRatio × derivStd. Late retention quality × dramatic pacing interaction.
r=+0.23, r_partial=+0.20. Post-peak trough / peak daily views. Sustained viewership after spike.
r=+0.31, r_partial=+0.22. Watch minutes per view on Day 1. Early engagement quality before algorithm kicks in.
r_partial=-0.23 (370), -0.12 (203). Fewer retention curve inflection points = smoother narrative = more views.

OUTCOME: revenue per mille. r=-0.01, CV +0.033. Lower RPM = broader (less monetizable) audience reach

r_partial=+0.36. Delayed peak (day 3+) shape: 31% hit 10M+, median 3.6M. Best trajectory category.
r=-0.52, r_partial=-0.50. Day1 views as fraction of 30-day total. STRONGEST trajectory signal after controlling for keep.
r_partial=-0.34. How well does exponential decay fit? Lower R² = more views. Viral = non-predictable trajectory.
Controlling for keep+maxCliff: viewAccel +0.32, narrativeMomentum +0.31, decayR2 -0.30, entropy +0.20. These 4 truly independent.
r=+0.32, r_partial=+0.19. Hook replay intensity (peak retention above 1.0). Top videos have 1.4+ replay peaks.
r=+0.30, r_partial=+0.25. How far through video before 70% retention threshold. Threshold-based narrative quality signal.
r=-0.26, r_partial=-0.19. Lower StdDev in middle 50% of curve = smoother narrative = more views.
r=0.632. BEST 3 NEW SIGNALS: z(kurtosis)+z(viewAccel)-z(day1share). Explains 40% of view variance from new discoveries alone.
r=0.622. keep + day1share = 39% view variance from just 2 signals. Day1share captures trajectory independently of quality.
r_partial=-0.27 (370). FEWER extreme derivative points = more views. Viral pacing is EVEN, not wildly variable.
r_partial=+0.20 (370). Flat middle section (low variance segments 3-7) predicts views. Consistent narrative holding.
subView<5%: 81% hit 10M+ (19.3M avg). subView<10%: 45% hit rate. Algorithm reach is THE differentiator (but outcome).
keep>=75 AND maxCliff>=0.15: 91/203 videos (45%), 36% hit 10M+, avg 5.2M. Practical pre-screening formula.
maxCliff>=0.25: 15 videos, 40% hit 10M+, avg 5.7M. Extreme dramatic moments are the power zone.
Engaged%: 92.5% for 5-10M tier vs 56.5% for 100M+. Viral videos reach casual viewers. Swipe 43.5% vs 7.5%.
Sub vs nonSub retention gap REVERSES by tier: 100M+ subs watch 4.2pts more, 10-50M subs watch 1pt LESS. Mega-viral engages loyal fans differently.
r=+0.24, r_partial=+0.15. More narrative transition words (but, however, then) = more views. Narrative structure signal.
r=-0.21, r_partial=-0.18. Concave cumulative view curve = growing interest over time. Viral videos have growth-oriented trajectories.
Ideal viral curve: 1.64x replay spike → 1.17 at 5% → 0.70 at 95%. Top-bottom gap GROWS toward tail (0.015 at 5% → 0.097 at 95%).
4-7 days between uploads: 33% hit 10M+. 1-3 days: only 11%. Too-fast uploading hurts. Algorithm needs breathing room.
After 10M+ hit: 33% next video also hits (avg 4.1M). After sub-1M miss: 14% hit rate (avg 1.1M). 2.4x momentum effect.
r_partial=+0.12. Using same visual Zeigarnik type as previous video slightly boosts views. Consistency of visual style helps.
r_partial=+0.18. shares/views × day7/day1 ratio. Proxy for organic viral spreading beyond algorithm.

avgWmGrowth r=-0.20, r_partial=-0.19. Watch quality declines faster for viral videos (audience dilution). Declining watch efficiency = GOOD sign (means broader reach).
BEST INDEPENDENT 3-SIGNAL SET: z(day3Growth)-z(first3Share)+z(deriv2Std). All three r>0.28 with views after double-control, mutual r<0.15. Three dimensions: acceleration, trajectory, pacing.

r_partial=+0.52. BEST COMPOSITE EVER. d2Std×(1-f3share)×log(d3g). Three orthogonal dimensions: pacing jerkiness × sustained trajectory × early acceleration. Beats narrative_momentum (+0.43).
r_partial=+0.50. endStartRatio×log(d3g). Payoff delivery × algorithmic acceleration. Two strong independent signals combined.
r=+0.52, r_partial=+0.42. endStartRatio×(1-f3share). Curve payoff quality × sustained trajectory. Strong on both raw and partial.
r=+0.26, r_partial=+0.36. d2Std/wmCV. Pacing dynamics normalized by watch quality consistency. Novel dimension.
r=+0.36, r_partial=+0.38. mean(curve)×(1-f3share). Simple but effective: overall retention × trajectory sustainability.
r=+0.03, r_partial=+0.20. Share/comment ratio. Content that SPREADS vs just gets discussed. Independent of keep.
r=-0.08, r_partial=+0.24. Total d2 squared of retention curve. Sign FLIPS after controlling for keep. Dynamic pacing after quality.
r=+0.01, r_partial=+0.21. Curvature in last 20% of curve. Only visible after controlling for keep. Conclusion dynamics matter.
ALL transcript timing signals (word gaps, speaking rate, pauses, breathlessness) have r_partial<0.12. Transcript timing does NOT predict views.
Titles with numbers: avg 1.1M vs 2.2M without (0.52x). r=-0.18. But r_partial=+0.02 — entirely explained by keep. Number titles have lower keep.
Titles starting with 'I': avg 1.2M vs 2.0M (0.59x). r=-0.13. But r_partial=-0.03 — mostly explained by keep. First-person framing hurts keep.
totalEngagement r_p=-0.20, engQuality r_p=-0.19. ALL per-view engagement metrics are INVERSE. Viral = passive viewers. Consistent across likes, comments, shares per view.
tripleComposite r_p=+0.508 after controlling for keep+retention+maxCliff+keepSq. SURVIVES ALL 4 model features. Captures entirely new dimension.
day3Growth alone: r_p=+0.482 after controlling keep+ret+cliff+keepSq. Strongest single NEW signal after 4-feature control. Algorithm acceleration is an independent dimension.
r=+0.57, r_p=+0.62. Sub conversions per subscriber viewer. VERY STRONG but OUTCOME. Viral videos convert non-sub audiences to subscribers efficiently.
r=+0.08, r_p=+0.17. Deviating from recent 3-video avg duration predicts MORE views. FORMAT NOVELTY helps. Counter-intuitive: consistency in length hurts.
r=+0.07, r_p=+0.16. View trend over last 3 videos (ascending momentum). Captures whether creator momentum is BUILDING vs declining.
45-60s: 28% hit 10M+, avg 2.5M, n=137. 30-45s: 16% hit, avg 1.6M, n=169. 15-30s: 2% hit, avg 0.7M, n=47. DEATH ZONE confirmed <30s.
After 4-feature control: tripleComposite +0.51, d3g +0.48, payoffEff +0.49, f3share -0.32, d2Std +0.27, esRatio +0.25. ALL survive. New signals are truly ORTHOGONAL to existing model.
subEfficiency (subs gained per sub viewer) r_p=+0.62. Highest engagement metric found. But circular: algorithm pushes to non-subs who then sub. Consequence not cause.

day3Growth is UNPREDICTABLE from all pre-upload features (max r=0.08). No content feature, curve feature, or sequence feature predicts early algorithmic acceleration. It IS the algorithm's decision signal.
first3Share IS partially predictable: prevKeep r=-0.29, keep r=-0.27, ret90 r=-0.26. Better quality (own + previous) → more sustained trajectory. Partially causal chain.
BEST 2-signal combo: -z(f3share)+z(d3g). r=0.587, r_partial=0.557 (controlling keep). Just 2 trajectory signals explain 35% of view variance.
BEST 3-signal combos (TIED): f3share+d3g+esRatio AND d2Std+f3share+d3g. Both r_partial=0.579. Three-signal model captures 34% of residual variance after keep.
z(keep)-z(f3share)+z(d3g)+z(esRatio): r=0.681. 46% of view variance from just 4 signals. Combines pre-upload quality with post-upload trajectory.
prevKeep → f3share: r=-0.29. Previous video's keep rate predicts THIS video's trajectory sustainability. Mechanism: good prior keep → algorithm gives longer runway.

Meta-finding: trajectory signals (d3g, f3share) are NOT content properties — they are ALGORITHM RESPONSES. d3g captures the algorithm's amplification decision. Cannot be used for pre-upload prediction.
r=-0.26, r_partial=-0.19. Higher deviation from exponential decay fit = fewer views. Clean exponential curves outperform messy ones. Complements inflection_count finding.
r=+0.11, r_partial=+0.31. Early like rate (likes/day1views) strongly predicts total views after controlling keep. Early engagement quality = algorithm signal. But partially outcome.

vzScore → f3share: r=+0.22. Higher visual Zeigarnik → MORE front-loaded trajectory. Strong hooks attract fast initial viewers but may not sustain. Implication: vz is a day1 signal, not a trajectory signal.

new_signal: Optimal WPS trajectory: START SLOW (3.2 wps at 0-20%, best ab=+0.008), then MED-FAST (4.7-4.8 wps at 20-100%)
60-80% at 4.7 wps = strongest pace effect (+0.030 avg_ab, 3.3M views). Slow (<3.2) = -0.027, 1.1M. 60-80% pace is critical.
Fast (>5.5 wps) is NEVER optimal — always Q3 (4.7-4.8) wins over Q4 (5.5). Don't rush, maintain medium-fast.
WPS×retention correlation strengthens late: r=+0.19 at 85-90% (strongest). Speed up slightly in final 20% for retention boost.
Decelerating pace = best retention (+0.006 avg_ab) but accelerating = most views (2.29M). Tension between retention and reach.
Top videos have MORE dramatic pauses at opening (0.81 >0.5s pauses vs 0.46 for bottom). Deliberate opening pacing.

r2_after: HIGH-ENERGY ACTION FRAMES are the #1 driver of retention peaks. At best above_baseline moments: action frames=28% (vs 8% at worst), high energy=42% (vs 2% at worst). avg_ab for action=+0.058.
PHYSICAL/SENSORY LANGUAGE drives retention. 'painful' 13x overrepresented in top quartile, 'jump' 11x, 'cold' 5x. Body-based words (feel, sweat, painful) >> technical words (fiber, carbon, solid).
END RECOVERY (85-95%) is the strongest single predictor of views: r=+0.506 with log(views). 'Weak hook, strong end' shape has highest median views (7.1M). Save biggest payoff for final 15%.
LOW-ENERGY STATIC FRAMES cause drops. At worst moments: low energy=40% (vs 14% at best). Text-only and static demonstration frames have lowest above_baseline (+0.011 and +0.014).
TECHNICAL/MATERIAL LANGUAGE kills retention. 'plate' 14x, 'fiber' 14x, 'carbon' 13x overrepresented in bottom quartile. Skip materials explanation, show result and reaction instead.
HOOK-TO-MID DROP is the strongest negative predictor: r=-0.511 with log(views). Most drops occur at 10-20% of video. Must deliver first payoff by 15-20% (seconds 7-10 for 45s Short).

No peaks at 30/60/90%. Shorts follow DECAY pattern. Key zones: Hook (0-10%, face+energy), Danger (10-20%, must deliver first payoff), Sustain (20-80%, flat, keep energy), Payoff (80-95%, biggest predictor of views).
Best retention: 30-40s (+0.012 above baseline). Most views: 50-60s (12.5M avg but -0.025 retention). Sweet spot: 35-45s balances both.
Above_baseline by visual type: reaction (+0.064) > action (+0.058) > talking_head (+0.026) > wide_shot (+0.025) > close_up (+0.024) > demonstration (+0.014) > text (+0.011).

Optimal hook (first 3s): face visible (84% of top videos), medium-high energy, text on screen (100%). Power words: 'apparently', 'happens', 'world', 'hours'. Avoid: 'honor', 'possible', 'ever', 'starting'.

Best shape: consistently_above (24 videos, 11.6M avg views). Highest median: weak_hook_strong_end (30 videos, 7.1M median). Worst: strong_hook_weak_mid (4 videos, 2.3M avg) — hooking then losing = worst strategy.

notes: r=0.215 vs log(views) p=0.002. Questions in first 30s of transcript. Audio proxy signal. Strongest audio predictor found.
r=0.151 vs log(views) p=0.031. Ratio of ALL-CAPS words in transcript. Intensity/emphasis proxy.
r=0.040 vs log(views), r=0.325 vs keep. "I" pronoun density — personal narrative drives swipe-in engagement.
r=-0.043 vs log(views), r=-0.299 vs retention. Low vocabulary diversity in first 30s = worse retention. Repetition hurts.
r=0.123 vs log(views) p=0.079, r=0.141 vs keep. "But" pivot word density per minute. Narrative pivots signal engagement.
r=-0.161 vs log(views) p=0.022. Presence of hook words in first 5s is NEGATIVELY correlated — surprising. Overused hooks may signal generic content.
r=-0.055 vs log(views), r=-0.155 vs keep. Superlative overload hurts. Less hyperbole = better hook engagement.
r=-0.036 vs log(views), r=0.174 vs retention, r=-0.126 vs keep. Faster speech = better retention but worse keep. Speed tradeoff.
r=-0.070 vs log(views), r=-0.143 vs retention. Full-video question density. Too many questions hurts retention.
r=0.076 vs log(views), r=0.176 vs retention. Accelerating speech (last 30s faster than first 30s) = better retention.
r=-0.271 vs log(views) p=0.0001. Average position of questions in video. Front-loaded questions = more views. STRONGEST narrative signal found.
r=0.201 vs log(views) p=0.004. Conflict word count in middle quarters (Q2+Q3). Mid-video tension predicts virality.
r=-0.179 vs log(views) p=0.011. Position of first "but/however" in transcript. Earlier pivots = more views.
r=0.168 vs log(views) p=0.016. Score 0-3: has_hook + has_conflict + has_resolution. Complete narrative arcs predict views.
r=0.017 vs log(views), r=0.260 vs retention. Hook word density concentrated in Q1 vs rest. Concentrated hooks = better retention.
r=0.138 vs log(views) p=0.050. Total narrative markers (hooks+conflicts+resolutions) per word. Denser narratives perform better.
r=-0.112 vs log(views), r=0.068 vs retention. Slope of conflict density across quarters. Negative = front-loaded tension (better for views).
r=0.192 vs log(views) p=0.006. SCIENCE category (experiment/robot/laser/magnetic/AI). 2.17x enriched in 10M+ videos. Top category signal.
r=0.192 vs log(views) p=0.006. MAKING category (made/built/create). 1.41x enriched in 10M+ videos. Core content identity.
r=0.154 vs log(views) p=0.028. INDESTRUCTIBLE category. 1.78x enriched in 10M+ videos. Strong virality signal.
r=0.132 vs log(views), r=-0.251 vs retention. SUPERHERO category: good for views but HURTS retention. Clickbait risk.
r=-0.079 vs log(views). MONEY category: 0.28x enriched in 10M+ (ANTI-signal). Money-focused titles underperform.
avg_logV=7.246 (n=4). MAKING+DANGER pair is the highest-performing category combination. Making dangerous things = virality formula.
avg_logV=7.087 (n=11). INDESTRUCTIBLE+MAKING pair. Second-best combo. Core Tyler brand.
avg_logV=7.037 (n=8). MAKING+SCIENCE pair. Third-best combo. Science builds perform well.
avg_logV=7.005 (n=6). MAKING+FOOD pair. Food builds cross into 10M territory.
r=0.078 vs log(views). DANGER category: 1.61x enriched in 10M+. Danger element boosts virality.
R²=0.022 from 10 pre-upload signals vs R²=0.219 from keep+retention. Pre-upload signals explain almost nothing. Performance is entirely content-driven.
r=-0.265 vs keep. Composite title score (power_words+caps+number+curiosity) NEGATIVELY predicts keep. Clickbait titles backfire on swipe engagement.
Contrast connectors (but/however/though/except/instead) per 100w. r=0.097 vs retention (ns). partial_r(dur)=0.157. vs keep=0.211 (p=.003). Strongest subtype for retention.
Surprise connectors (wait/actually/turns out/surprisingly) per 100w. r=0.083 vs retention (ns). vs keep=0.163 (p=.02).
Forward connectors (so/then/next/now) per 100w. r=0.025 vs retention (ns). vs keep=0.066 (ns). DEAD for both metrics.
Causal connectors (because/since/therefore/means). r=-0.085 vs retention (ns). vs keep=0.137 (ns). NEGATIVE trend — explaining causality may hurt retention.

Expanded 21-word connector density vs retention. r=0.087 (ns). partial_r(dur)=0.162 (marginal). vs keep=0.295 (p<.001). CONFIRMS: connectors predict KEEP not retention. Q1→Q5: 82.3%→84.0% ret, 70.7%→76.4% keep.
Hook-concept word reappearance in final 20% (continuous fraction). r=0.07 vs end-retention (ns). partial_r(dur)=0.175. vs retention=0.005 (ns). vs keep=0.053 (ns). 71% callback rate. DEAD SIGNAL.
Binary callback (any hook concept in final 20%). r=-0.006 vs end-retention (ns). vs ret=-0.087 (ns). vs keep=0.070 (ns). Callback YES: ret=82.1%, NO: ret=83.7%. NOT predictive.

Position-controlled retention delta by segment function: hook(-0.005), tension(+0.004), pivot(-0.003), payoff(+0.001), build(-0.008), test(+0.009). ALL p>0.20. No segment type locally predicts retention.

Per-video segment-type fractions all NEGATIVELY correlate with retention (r=-0.19 to -0.26) but POSITIVELY with keep (r=+0.04 to +0.30). ALL vanish after duration control. PURE DURATION CONFOUND — shorter videos have more function words AND higher retention.
Late above-baseline (80-100% of curve vs avg decay). r=0.926 vs retention, r=0.313 vs keep, r=0.321 vs log_views. BEST VIEW PREDICTOR from curve features. End-strong videos get more views.
Above-baseline range (max-min deviation from avg curve). r=-0.41 vs keep, r=-0.01 vs retention, r=-0.05 vs views. VOLATILE curves hurt keep. Consistent performance = better swipe-through.

At above-baseline moments: experiential words enriched (hurt 4.0x, crazy 4.0x, feel 3.3x, getting 3.3x, surprised 3.0x, break 2.8x). At below-baseline: procedural words (water 0.24x, back 0.29x, working 0.32x, which 0.38x, finally 0.43x, your 0.45x, video 0.47x).

Word categories at above-baseline: pivot words 1.15x enriched. reveal words DEPLETED 0.69x (reveals come at drops!). action/connector/question ~1.0x (neutral). KEY: reveals happen when retention is dropping, not rising.

Within-video local correlations with above-baseline: pivot(+0.011, ns), action(-0.023, ns), connector(+0.003, ns), info_density(-0.024, ns). NO word category reliably predicts local above-baseline. Retention driven by visual/audio, not transcript at local level.
r=0.696 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Non-subscriber view fraction. partial_r=0.609 (controlling keep+ret). STRONGEST SIGNAL FOUND. Algorithm distribution proxy.
r=-0.394 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Biggest single retention drop. partial_r=-0.315. Smoother curve = more views, independent of keep/retention.
r=-0.371 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Fraction of views on day 1. partial_r=-0.321. SLOW STARTERS WIN — algorithmic distribution builds over days.
r=-0.292 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Likes per 1000 views. partial_r=-0.235. Higher like ratio = fewer views. Fans like, viral viewers don't.
r=-0.267 vs log(views) p=0.0001. retention_variation from YouTube analytics. partial_r=-0.210. Smoother retention curves → more views.
r=-0.234 vs log(views) p=0.0008. partial_r=-0.203. Retention at 10% / start. Counter-intuitive: videos with LARGER initial drop get more views.
r=0.193 vs log(views) p=0.006. Retention at 50% / retention at 25%. Good mid-body hold predicts views.
r=-0.224 vs log(views), r=-0.478 vs keep, r=-0.327 vs retention. Gap between sub and non-sub retention. Huge gap = content only works for existing fans.
r=-0.224 vs log(views) p=0.001. partial_r=-0.154. First 3 days as fraction of total views. Confirms slow-starter pattern from day1_frac.
r=0.154 vs log(views), r=0.354 vs keep. End retention vs mid retention. Strong endings boost keep rate — rewatch/save signal.
r=-0.048 vs log(views), r=0.368 vs retention. Engaged view fraction. Strongly predicts retention but not raw views.
r=-0.009 vs log(views), r=0.483 vs keep. Revenue per 1000 views. Monetizable attention correlates with keep, not views.
r=-0.199 vs log(views) p=0.005. partial_r=-0.110. Comments per 1000 views. Like like_ratio — engaged communities comment more per view.
r=0.138 vs log(views) p=0.050. Day 2 / Day 1 view growth ratio. Growth after day 1 predicts total views.
r=0.050 vs log(views), r=-0.389 vs retention. Swipe-away rate hurts retention dramatically but doesn't kill views directly.
r=0.246 vs log(views), r=0.832 vs retention. Retention at video midpoint. Nearly identical to avg retention (redundant but validates curve shape).
r=-0.486 vs log(views). synergy=+0.056. keep × biggest_drop interaction — combined effect stronger than either alone.
r=0.447 vs log(views). synergy=+0.017. keep × retention multiplicative interaction.
r=0.236 vs log(views) p=0.001. synergy=+0.021. Questions in first 30s × mid-video conflict count. Combined curiosity signal.
r=0.338 vs log(views) p<0.001. arc_completeness × reach_ratio. Complete arcs that reach non-subscribers = virality formula.
r=0.306 vs log(views) p<0.001. reach_ratio / (keep/100). Algorithmic reach normalized by hook quality.
r=0.264 vs log(views) p=0.0001. keep × vz_score / 10. Hook quality composite.
r=0.234 vs log(views) p=0.001. first_30s_questions × (1 - avg_question_pos). Front-loaded curiosity signal.
r=0.221 vs log(views) p=0.002. (1 - first_pivot_pos) × conflict_q2q3. Fast pivots with high conflict = views.
reach_ratio >= 0.89: Δmean_logV=+1.131 (p<0.001). MASSIVE threshold effect — crossing median reach = 13.5x more views.
keep >= 75.7: Δmean_logV=+0.653 (p<0.001). Crossing median keep = 4.5x more views.
reach_ratio >= 0.94: Δmean_logV=+1.183 (p<0.001). Top quartile reach = 15.2x more views than bottom 75%.
r=0.199 vs log(views) p=0.005. HOOK_HOLD retention shape (big drop then flat body). 27 videos. avg_logV=6.672 vs 6.254 for smooth_decline.
r=0.348 vs log(views) p<0.001. Ratio of hook drop to body drop in retention curve. Hook hard, hold flat = formula.
r=0.363 vs log(views) p<0.001. Counter-intuitive: BIGGER early retention drops = MORE views. Algorithm rewards committed watchers.
r=0.233 vs log(views) p=0.001. Positive curve concavity (drops decelerating). Slowing drops = more views.
r=-0.312 vs log(views) p<0.001. Less body drop = more views. Body retention is what the algorithm optimizes for.
r=0.326 vs log(views) p<0.001. Bigger hook drops = more views. Confirms HOOK_HOLD pattern — losing viewers early is OK.
r=-0.247 vs log(views) p<0.001. Smaller mid-section range = more views. Flat middle = good.
r=0.524 vs keep. avg_concavity strongly predicts keep — videos where drops slow down get more swipe-in.
r=-0.176 vs log(views) p=0.012. Novelty per minute. High novelty density in short videos doesn't help.

First-person (I/my/me) density in last 20% → above-baseline late. r=+0.18 (p=.009). Personal language at end helps retention.
Experiential words in last 20% → above-baseline late. r=+0.155 (p=.028). Sensation language at end supports retention.

Strong-ending (top25% ab_late): ret=91.2%, keep=77.7%, views=2.9M. Weak: ret=71.5%, keep=72.4%, views=838K. 3.5x view difference. Signature: fewer words, more experiential, more first-person, less you/your.
r=-0.144 vs log(views) p=0.041. Negative sentiment slope across video segments. Decreasing negativity over time predicts views.
r=0.142 vs log(views) p=0.043. Position of peak resolution words. Later resolution = more views. Delayed gratification.
r=0.139 vs log(views) p=0.048. Number of sentiment swings (sign changes). More emotional variety = more views.
r=-0.021 vs log(views), r=0.174 vs retention. Speech pace variance across segments. Consistent pace helps retention.
r=-0.169 vs log(views) p=0.016. Subscriber churn ratio (lost/gained). Higher churn = fewer views. Quality indicator.
r=-0.292 vs log(views) p<0.001. Total engagements (likes+shares+comments+subs) per view. Confirms: engaged communities ≠ viral reach.
r=0.483 vs keep. Revenue per mille strongly correlates with keep rate. Monetizable attention = hook quality.
r=0.066 vs log(views), r=-0.275 vs retention. Share-to-like ratio. Higher sharing = worse retention (viewers share and leave).

R²=0.630 from 5 features: reach_ratio + day1_frac + hook_body_ratio + body_drop + first_30s_questions. Parsimonious and powerful.
R²=0.585 from 3 features: reach_ratio + day1_frac + body_drop. Three signals explain 59% of view variance.

ΔR²=+0.081 when adding day1_frac to keep+retention. Second-best incremental signal. Slow starts = algorithmic distribution.

r=0.169 vs log(views) p=0.016. 1 - sub_lost/sub_gained. Higher quality = more views. Videos that make subs stay perform better.

Physical sensation words (hurt/pain/heavy/hot/cold/burning/shaking). r=+0.15 vs retention (p=.03). partial_r(dur)=+0.15. r=-0.046 vs keep.

Within-video local experiential density → above-baseline: mean_r=+0.013 (ns). 52% positive. GLOBAL signal (creator style), NOT local predictor. Experiential videos retain better overall, but placing experiential words at specific seconds doesn't spike retention.
BEST retention model: duration+exp_minus_proc+pivot+wps+exp+proc. CV R²=0.347 (10-seed mean=0.333±0.029, 10/10 positive). vs previous best 0.297. Delta=+0.077. Exp signal adds most predictive power.

(Exp-proc) × pivot interaction. r=+0.356 vs retention (p<.001). partial_r(dur)=+0.314. Strongest retention interaction found. Pivot words + experiential language = peak retention recipe.
r=0.337 vs log(views) p<0.001. Day 7 views / Day 1 views. Growth across first week predicts total views. Algorithm momentum signal.
r=0.204 vs log(views) p=0.004, r=0.243 vs retention. Second peak / first peak after day 7. Re-engagement signal.
r=-0.062 vs log(views), r=0.285 vs retention. Post-peak view decay rate (log slope). Slower decay = better retention.
r=0.109 vs log(views), r=0.213 vs retention. Day of peak views. Later peaks suggest algorithmic pickup over time.

r=-0.192 vs keep. Title uppercase ratio HURTS keep. ALL CAPS in titles backfires on swipe engagement.

retention×action_density synergy = +0.059 R². Action verbs amplify retention's effect on views.

r=-0.371 vs log(views). STRONGEST trajectory signal. High day1_pct = flash-in-the-pan. Top videos: 2-3% on day1, bottom: 9-11%. Subs-only vs algorithm-pushed.
r=+0.346 vs log(views). Raw day-1 volume still matters — a strong absolute launch helps even if proportionally small.
r=+0.336 vs log(views). Slow decay ratio (day7/day1) strongly predicts total views. Top videos: 0.7-17× day1 at day7. Bottom: 0.06-0.18×.
r=+0.306 vs log(views). Views INCREASING day1→3 predicts virality. Top: 3-13× growth. Bottom: 0.3-0.5× decay. Algorithm amplification signature.
r=+0.310 vs log(views), r=+0.269 vs keep. Day-1 watch-minutes-per-view predicts both views AND keep. Early engagement quality = algorithm signal.
r=+0.296 vs log(views), r=+0.209 vs keep, r=+0.236 vs retention. Longer half-life predicts all three outcomes. Sustained attention = compound growth.
r=-0.390 vs retention. STRONGEST retention anti-signal from trajectory. Frontloaded views = low retention. Videos that spread viewership over time retain better.
r=+0.311 vs log(views). Day30/day1 ratio = longevity signal. Evergreen content keeps delivering views weeks later.
MEGA-HIT TRAJECTORY: peak day 3-6, acceleration 3-13×, half-life 2-3 days, long-tail 27-41%. FLOP: peak day 2, acceleration 0.3×, half-life 1 day, long-tail 51-60%.
has_second_peak and is_frontloaded are ~uncorrelated with views (r≈0). Continuous decay-rate metrics outperform binary shape categories. But second_peak helps retention (r=+0.22).
r=-0.847 vs log(views), p<0.0001. STRONGEST SIGNAL IN ENTIRE DATASET. High subscriber view fraction = fewer total views. Reach beyond subs is what drives views. Q4-Q1 gap = ~40× views.
r=+0.696 vs log(views), p<0.0001. Mirror of sub_view_ratio. Non-subscriber reach fraction is the #2 strongest signal. Viral = reaching beyond your base.
r=-0.292 vs log(views), p<0.0001. High like_rate = FEWER views. Counterintuitive: smaller videos have more engaged core. Viral dilutes per-view engagement.
r=-0.297 vs log(views), p<0.0001. Weighted engagement score negatively predicts views. Same dilution effect — viral reach trades off vs per-view engagement.
r=-0.267 vs log(views), p=0.0001. Lower retention variation = more views. Smooth, consistent retention curve beats spiky engagement.
r=+0.260 vs log(views), r=+0.410 vs retention. High likes-to-comments ratio = broad passive approval. Low ratio = divisive/niche content.
r=-0.478 vs keep, r=-0.327 vs retention. When subs watch much longer than non-subs = fan service underperforming with general audience. Best videos have EQUAL sub/nonsub retention.
r=-0.199 vs log(views). Q1 (lowest comment rate) avg 5.3M views vs Q4 (highest) 760K — 7× gap. Comment-heavy = niche.
rpm r≈0 vs views but r=+0.477 vs keep. Higher RPM videos have better retention metrics but don't necessarily get more views. Quality without reach.

PATTERN: Per-view engagement metrics (like_rate, comment_rate, engagement_score) ALL negatively correlate with views. Viral reach dilutes engagement rates. Don't optimize for engagement rate — optimize for reach.

r=-0.396 vs log(views). STRONGEST retention shape signal. Videos where second half retains proportionally more audience get far more views. Balanced curves beat front-loaded.
r=+0.365 vs log(views). Consistent flat body retention (low variance 10%-80%) strongly predicts views. The BODY matters more than the hook.

r=+0.320 vs log(views), r=+0.958 vs retention. End retention (at 80% mark) strongly predicts both views and average retention.
r=-0.291 vs log(views). Gentler early slope (less steep drop in first 10%) correlates with more views. But individual ret_at_10pct is only r=+0.06.
r=-0.389 vs keep. END DROP is the #1 keep predictor from retention shapes. Videos where audience bails at ending get lower keep. Don't let videos cliff at the end.
has_end_bump r=+0.289 vs keep. Re-engagement bumps in last 20% predict higher keep. Payoff/reveal at end brings people back.
ret_at_10pct r=+0.06, ret_at_3pct r=+0.12 vs views. Early hook retention is a WEAK predictor of total views. Body and endgame matter far more.
Cluster 3 (51 videos, 12.4M avg): loses 31% in first 10% then HOLDS. Body slope nearly flat. Includes INDESTRUCTIBLE (285M), MAGNET shoes (55M). THE winning shape.
Cluster 5 (15 videos, 1.6M avg): BEST early retention (0.807 at 10%) but end cliff (0.499→0.277). Great hook can't save bad ending. Hook obsession is a trap.
STRATEGIC: Don't obsess over hook — obsess over body and ending. Body signals (stability r=0.365, slope r=0.362) dwarf hook signals (ret_10pct r=0.06) for predicting views.

CUMULATIVE: ~170 new entries, tree v34, 95+ nodes. Best model CV R²=0.358, leave-10%-out R²=0.453. 8 key breakthroughs: sensory_compound, negative_valence, neg×sensory, tension_proxy, within-video null, retention-views paradox, tense_diversity, setup_density.
All 8 pre-theorized interaction hypotheses FAILED (none p<0.05). No magic combos — body_stability×MAKING, conflict×stability, etc. are all additive, not multiplicative.
arc_score × questions_first30s: dR²=+3.69%, p=0.004. BEST CONTROLLABLE SYNERGY. Strong narrative arc + question hooks = super-additive views. Do BOTH.
keep × total_conflict: dR²=+3.48%, p=0.003. High keep + high conflict = multiplicative. Conflict only pays off if the hook gets people in first.
keep × arc_score: dR²=+3.41%, p=0.003. Good hook + complete narrative arc = super-additive. Story payoff multiplies the hook's effect.
hook_pace × arc_score: beta=-0.120, dR²=+3.36%, p=0.007. ANTAGONISTIC. Fast hooks CANCEL narrative arc benefits. These are COMPETING strategies, not complementary.
hook_pace × total_conflict: beta=-0.103, dR²=+2.12%, p=0.030. Fast pace undermines conflict payoff. Slow, deliberate delivery lets conflict land.
Of 105 pairs tested, only 19 had significant interactions. Most content signals contribute INDEPENDENTLY. No need for complex interaction models — additive is sufficient.
Top 5 interactions by dR² all involve trajectory signals (day1_views, day7_vs_day1, day1_pct). But these are outcomes, not controllable. Content interactions are weaker but actionable.
TWO COMPETING STRATEGIES: (A) Slow hook + narrative arc + conflict density, or (B) Fast hook + simple content. Mixing A and B cancels benefits. Pick one lane.
